title: issue trying to prove equivalency with equiv_struct
body:
I am trying to prove equivalency with a blackbox submodule and found a somewhat weird behavior.
There are also considerable differences between older commits and HEAD.

## Steps to reproduce the issue

a.v:
```
module a(input a, input b, output c, output d);
wire g;
add add(.a(a), .b(b), .c(c), .d(g));
assign d = g & 1;
endmodule
```

b.v:
```
module a(input a, input b, output c, output d);
wire f;
add add(.a(a), .b(b), .c(c), .d(f));
assign d = f & 1;
endmodule
```

run:
```
yosys -p "read_verilog -sv ./a.v; hierarchy -auto-top; rename -top gold; design -stash gold; read_verilog -sv ./b.v; hierarchy -auto-top; rename -top gate; design -stash gate; design -copy-from gold -as gold gold; design -copy-from gate -as gate gate; equiv_make gold gate equiv; hierarchy -top equiv; flatten; equiv_simple; equiv_struct; equiv_simple;  equiv_status;"
```

## Expected behavior

All equiv cells proven.

## Actual behavior

```
Unproven $equiv $auto$equiv_make.cc:241:find_same_wires$4: \d_gold \d_gate
```

Now the part that I think is weirder is using a.v as gold and gate (simple net name change)

1) successful verification in Yosys 0.7+681 (git sha1 ed1f0b25, clang 6.0.1 -fPIC -Os)
2) fails in Yosys 0.8+23 (git sha1 db676957, clang 6.0.1 -fPIC -Os)

I am not sure this is intended behavior that got fixed recently or not. 
However, it would be nice to be able to structurally verify in both cases (same and different net names).
code_blocks: yosys -p "read_verilog -sv ./a.v; hierarchy -auto-top; rename -top gold; design -stash gold; read_verilog -sv ./b.v; hierarchy -auto-top; rename -top gate; design -stash gate; design -copy-from gold -as gold gold; design -copy-from gate -as gate gate; equiv_make gold gate equiv; hierarchy -top equiv; flatten; equiv_simple; equiv_struct; equiv_simple;  equiv_status;"
